characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: A 24-year-old slacker and aspiring cartoonist who is forced to put up with his nerdy younger brother. He works at a waterbed store, a job he hates. Aww Look They Really Do Love Each Other: Shockingly averted. In episode 18, when Kevin is forced to go to Wyoming to live with his parents, almost everyone finds a reason to miss Kevin. The only one who doesn't at any point is Andy, in fact no mention is made even once of him missing Kevin. What finally convinces Andy to let Kevin come back isn't him even actually missing Kevin, it's Mr. French paying Andy $5000. Brilliant, but Lazy: He's a good cartoonist, but he's also a slacker who spends a lot of his time on the couch drinking beer. He works harder than most do, as he does actually send off his cartoons to be published, but he's easily discouraged when he gets back rejection letters, something that's common even for professionals. Butt-Monkey: He becomes aButt-Monkeyin one episode where he meets a famous celebrity and promises to meet up with her during the Grammys but suffers through aTrauma Conga Linewhen his plans to meet up with her don't go as planned. The Casanova: What he tries to be. He's a bit too much of a pervert to get it to work to his favor, especially when he treats Gwen like a last minute hook up or tries to make a few innuendos at Kevin's teacher.

characters.name: Andy French
characters.description: A 24-year-old slacker and aspiring cartoonist who is forced to put up with his nerdy younger brother. He works at a waterbed store, a job he hates. Aww Look They Really Do Love Each Other: Shockingly averted. In episode 18, when Kevin is forced to go to Wyoming to live with his parents, almost everyone finds a reason to miss Kevin. The only one who doesn't at any point is Andy, in fact no mention is made even once of him missing Kevin. What finally convinces Andy to let Kevin come back isn't him even actually missing Kevin, it's Mr. French paying Andy $5000. Brilliant, but Lazy: He's a good cartoonist, but he's also a slacker who spends a lot of his time on the couch drinking beer. He works harder than most do, as he does actually send off his cartoons to be published, but he's easily discouraged when he gets back rejection letters, something that's common even for professionals. Butt-Monkey: He becomes aButt-Monkeyin one episode where he meets a famous celebrity and promises to meet up with her during the Grammys but suffers through aTrauma Conga Linewhen his plans to meet up with her don't go as planned. The Casanova: What he tries to be. He's a bit too much of a pervert to get it to work to his favor, especially when he treats Gwen like a last minute hook up or tries to make a few innuendos at Kevin's teacher.

characters.name: Kevin French
characters.description: Andy's 17-year-old brother, a massive nerd who wants to go to Yale. He moves in with Andy after their parents move to Wyoming. Annoying Younger Sibling: He's this to Andy. Big Brother Worship: Despite them being almost complete opposites, Kevin admits that Andy is the "Coolest person he knows". Andy is somewhat astonished that Kevin feels that way. Played with in that when Andy is being slobbish, Kevin can't stand him and views him as a bit of a loser. But when Andy shows his more nobler qualities, Kevin is always ready to praise him. Butt-Monkey: He gets a crappy birthday in his birthday episode. Even Andy's attempts to cheer him up make him feel even more depressed and unhappy. The end of the episode reveals that the initial crappiness of his birthday had actually set off a serious bout of homesickness, causing his depression.In the SAT episode, he gets hit quite a lot, including a few blows to the groin. He also gets beaten up by a bunch of volleyball girls, later a group of jocks who also give him a swirlie, and even later pushes his friends' berserk buttons to get them to beat him up. They keep going even after he asks them to stop. In the SAT episode, he gets hit quite a lot, including a few blows to the groin. He also gets beaten up by a bunch of volleyball girls, later a group of jocks who also give him a swirlie, and even later pushes his friends' berserk buttons to get them to beat him up. They keep going even after he asks them to stop.

characters.name: Jim Kuback
characters.description: Andy's best friend since high school, who now lives with him in Mission Hill. Despite being just as much of a slacker as Andy, he has a cushy job at an advertising agency. Berserk Button: Of the times Jim has gotten angry, it's when someone has sold out or messed with his food. In the series proper, he only really lost his cool with Andy when Andy became a sell out on The Real World or a bad skeezy salesman when Andy became Ron's second in command. In the unaired "Freaky Weekend" episode, his patience finally wore thin when someone ate the last of the food item he wanted to order at a fast food place so he couldn't get it. Best Friend: To Andy, and has been so for many years. They even went to the same college! Big Ol' Unibrow: He has a huge unibrow. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: He's a major slacker, but he happens to be good with computers. Everyone at the ad agency treats Jim's opinions and his feelings as vitally important, basically giving Jim as much power as the boss, and the power to hire and fire. He eventually uses this to his advantage, kicking out a new employee, and the snooty employment officer who hired him, to have him replaced with Andy.

characters.name: Posey Tyler
characters.description: One of Andy's roommates in the apartment. She's a hippie who's concerned with things like chakras and organic foods. Beware the Nice Ones: Has a real mean streak thats usually covered by her ditzy personality but surfaces when she feels particularly spiteful.Example: When she and Andy are accosted by a pimp who believes Posey is turning tricks after a misprint in her advertisement for her massage therapy, the pimp nearly throws Andy off the roof of the building but suddenly throws out his back as he is about to do so. Posey uses her masseuse training to fix his backbefore kicking him off the roof of the building.Andy: What the hell was that about?Posey:I didn't want him to fall off the roof and not feel it. Example: When she and Andy are accosted by a pimp who believes Posey is turning tricks after a misprint in her advertisement for her massage therapy, the pimp nearly throws Andy off the roof of the building but suddenly throws out his back as he is about to do so. Posey uses her masseuse training to fix his backbefore kicking him off the roof of the building. The Ditz: Not exactly the brightest of the apartment residents. Even the Girls Want Her: When Gwen is somewhat drunk, she and Posey dance together very sensually.

characters.name: Stogie
characters.description: The Alcoholic: Andy and Kevin's parents had to spray the liquor cabinet with dog repellent in order to keep him away. Dead Guy on Display: A pet variation — in the script for the unfinished "Death Of a Yale Man", Kevin has anImagine Spotset in aBad Futurecaused by him failing to get into Yale, where everyone is aFuture Loser, except Stogie, who hasdied, with his taxidermied corpse still kept in the apartment. Dogs Are Dumb: Though in this case it's implied to be because of minor brain damage. Extreme Omnivore: He'll eat anything, including a TV remote and couch cushions that have had burger grease dripped on them. The only line he draws is the "dog food" that Andy gives him in one episode, which is implied to be madefromdogs. Person of Mass Destruction: He chews up everything he can get his paws on. Posey outright declares him a menace when she proclaims "There are dark forces at work within him."

characters.name: Gus Duncz
characters.description: Another of the neighbors. He's a gruff, elderly gay man in his late sixties who runs the localGreasy Spoondiner. Bad "Bad Acting": When he was acting as the star ofThe Man From Pluto, he was unable to read the more sophisticated dialogue that Wally wrote, or hide his obvious New York accent.Gus:People of Oith, I greet yous from the Galastic Federation.And then there was hisDelayed Reactionupon getting shot.Wally:Gus! He just shot you! Your bulb is on, you're angry!Gus:Oh yeah?...RRRR! And then there was hisDelayed Reactionupon getting shot.Wally:Gus! He just shot you! Your bulb is on, you're angry!Gus:Oh yeah?...RRRR! Blush Sticker: After Andy playfully calls him a dirty tramp for giving him a recommendation for his job interview, he sports a pair of these and is adorably bashful. Brooklyn Rage: Gus is easily prone to anger and speaks with a heavy New York accent.

characters.name: Wally Langford
characters.description: Gus' partner. Wally is a former cinematographer/film director who now works as the projectionist at a movie theater that only plays classic films. Berserk Button: For how much of a stretch of "Berserk" a more mellow guy like Wally can get. Outside of the occasional couple's spat with Gus which is more some minor gripes, Wally really doesn't like art being tampered with. He seems the most exasperated with Kevin when Kevin had difficulty understanding old classic cinema, and he was distraught when Andy casually got his fingerprints all over the vinyl Wally lent Andy in "Pretty in Pink". Camp Gay: Downplayed. He's a bit more feminine than hisManly Gayboyfriend, but he's not completely over-the-top. "Prissy" would be a better word to describe him. In fact, the idea of dressing in revealing, flamboyant hotpants is repugnant to him, and to Gus as well.Gus:No self respectin' gay man would be caught dead in them trunks.Wally:I would die TWICE if anyone saw me wearing those. Death Glare: He gives one to Kevin after he went around all of Mission Hill promoting Wally's filmThe Man From Pluto. Wally leaves the theater after everyone starts laughing at how bad the movie is, and after Kevin and the rest of the gang try to stop him from leaving, Wally'sDeath Glarestops them all cold in their tracks. Happily Married: Gay marriage hadn't been legalized when the show was made, but he and Gus are this trope in everything but the paperwork, having been together since the 1950's, and act exactly like an old married couple, because that's essentially what they are.

characters.name: Carlos Hernandez
characters.description: A painter and father who lives with his wife Natalie, and their child, Baby Nameless. Nice Guy: Both he and his wife are very nice and sweet to Andy and his friends. Papa Wolf: When he believes that a giant snake has swallowed his baby, he readily leaps off the roof and onto a flag pole to get to it. Starving Artist: Averted. Unlike Andy, who wants to be a cartoonist but works in a waterbed store, Carlos is a painter whose artwork is featured in galleries. Comedically, when Andy is looking for work and tried to be a caricature artist, Carlos said Andy shouldn't prostitute his art and a true artist would rather starve. Cue Gus giving Carlos a big burger meal and Carlos sliding the bill to Natalie.

characters.name: Natalie Leibowitz-Hernandez
characters.description: She lives in the apartment with her husband, Carlos, and their child, Baby Nameless. Animal Wrongs Group: She's a part of one. They break into an animal testing lab to save the animals in there, some of which end up missing the lab, like the poor cigarette-addicted monkey who is now forced to listen to a kumbaya playing hippie. Granola Girl: A different one from Posey. Instead of being organic and spiritual, Natalie concerns herself with animal rights, and refuses to give her child a name to avoid conforming to gender norms. Mama Bear: Like her husband she became very protective of her baby when she thinks a giant snake consumed him.Natalie: Screw activism, that slimy bastard's gonna die! No Celebrities Were Harmed: Modeled after writer and historianLois Leveen.

characters.name: Baby Nameless
characters.description: The infant child of Natalie and Carlos, who has yet to be given an actual name, to avoid "affirming gender norms". Ambiguous Gender: Deliberatly invoked by their parents, who hasn't named their child yet to avoid conforming to gender norms at such a young age. Cheerful Child: A very happy and energetic baby, to the point that they never even cry on-screen. Pet Monstrosity: Ends up bonding with the boa constrictor Natalie rescued from an animal lab in "Kevin's Birthday" which becomes their pet snake. The Unintelligible: Doesn't speak yet, and communicates through typical baby babble and giggling.

characters.name: Gwen
characters.description: Andy's on-again off-again girlfriend and co-worker at the waterbed store. Abhorrent Admirer: Toby is implied to have a crush on her, to her discomfort. Depending on the Writer: Her relationship with Andy and general personality tends to vary from episode to episode. She can either be laid-back and cool, or snarky and easily annoyed. One more justifiable example is the first part of Unemployment, where she is also annoyed about being low on cash since the water bed world got shut down. When Andy and Kevin can't afford the toll, she ditches them because of their annoying fighting and she was the one who bought the tickets, why SHOULD she have to pay for everything? Even the Girls Want Her: In the unaired "Pretty in Pink" episode, she and Posey dance together very sensually. Ms. Fanservice: As far as the art style allows for one anyway.

characters.name: Toby Mundorf
characters.description: One of Kevin's new friends upon moving to Mission Hill. He's a fat, cowardly nerd with a highly overprotective mother. Beware the Nice Ones: In an online game, Kevin sold Toby's viking avatar into slavery, after Toby fell asleep when he was supposed to be defending their fortress. When Toby finds out, he swears revenge on Kevin. When Kevin admits to destroying his computer for the SAT code (this was a ploy to get Kevin beaten up more than he already was), Toby comes in swinging and doesn't stop even when Kevin begs him to. Butt-Monkey: He gets chased by a rabid dog in one episode and spends most of the episode trying to call for help but to no avail. Creator Cameo: He is voiced by one of the show's co-creators. Fat Best Friend: He's this to Kevin and George.

characters.name: George Bang
characters.description: Kevin's other friend in Mission Hill. He's a Korean nerd whose father runs the local convenience store, where he occasionally works. Asian and Nerdy: He's Korean, and he's into the same nerdy sci-fi as Kevin and Toby. Asian Store-Owner: George's father runs the local convenience store, where George also occasionally works. Butt-Monkey: He gets pushed around by everyone around him. Even his best friends aren't above mistreating him. Even his own computer gets in on it! Creator Cameo: He is voiced by one of the show's co-creators.

characters.name: Ron
characters.description: The owner of the waterbed store where Andy and Gwen work. He's an angry Armenian who mistreats his employees and embezzles money. Abusive Parents: He offhandedly mentions that he's abandoned his mentally ill son. Angrish: Whenever he's particularly angry, he explodes into a combination of this and his country's native language. Bad Boss: He overworks his employees, thinks lowly of women, and generally spends as little time actually working as possible. He either leaves to go to a strip joint or goes into his office to do cocaine. Basement Dweller: A possibly inverted case where he still lives with his mother, but he's clearly the one in charge, and he doesn't treat her well.

characters.name: Mr. and Mrs. French
characters.description: Andy and Kevin's parents, who have moved to Wyoming, leaving Kevin and Stogie in Andy's care. The Bore: Mr. French is an incredibly unemotional and boring man. Covert Pervert: In the PTA episode, Kevin calls them to get them to browbeat Andy into helping Kevin. Cue Mr. French walking over to a hot tub where Mrs. French is naked, and Mr. French disrobing to show he's getting in completely naked as well.Episode 18's whole conflict would not have happened if not for this, arguably. Kevin didn't know they were filming a pornographic film when he barged in to tell the people in the apartment to keep the noise down. But, Mr. French was the one who rented pornographic tapes. When Andy asks how they even GOT a tape of that film, they do not tell him the truth. Episode 18's whole conflict would not have happened if not for this, arguably. Kevin didn't know they were filming a pornographic film when he barged in to tell the people in the apartment to keep the noise down. But, Mr. French was the one who rented pornographic tapes. When Andy asks how they even GOT a tape of that film, they do not tell him the truth. Entertainingly Wrong: A lot of the comedy from episode 18, where the French parents are more active in the plot, comes from Mrs. French believing Andy is beside himself with grief at multiple points because Kevin is going to Wyoming. She even cries when Andy calls her up to say that he misses Kevin. Not knowing that he's acting and that Mr. French paid Andy several thousand dollars.

characters.name: Eunice "Weirdie" Eulmeyer
characters.description: A creepy female classmate of Kevin's who he tries to get romantically involved with after he finds out her father, one of the men involved in the development of the hydrogen bomb, could be his ticket to a Yale recommendation. Alliterative Name: Her actual name is Eunice Eulmeyer. Even Nerds Have Standards: She may look like one, but even she thinks Kevin is a big dork. Though she did seem to havesomefeelings for him, even while acting under the correct assumption that he was just using her. Hidden Depths: When she and Kevin infiltrate an army base to get back her father's letter of recommendation to Yale, she's shown to be a surprisingly good actor and quick thinker. She first pretends to be a frightened child looking for her scientist father in order to gain access to the room where the files are in. Afterwards, when a guard is about to catch them in there, she starts making out with Kevin and acting like a sexy girlfriend, in order to say that they went in there on a dare. Insult of Endearment: She calls Kevin "Water Balloon" and "Chimp" for some reason, and still calls him that after they become friends.

characters.name: Griffo and C-Dog
characters.description: Two bullies that go to Kevin's school. Their real names are Jonathan Griffin and Christopher Dodge. Disproportionate Retribution: The two of them are arrested for attempting to burn down the convenience store and murder Kevin, and are set to get 25 to life in prison. However, they didn't start the fire and didn't try to kill Kevin, all they did was steal $53 from the register. Kevin had to admit in court the embarrassing accident that lead to their wrongful conviction. Everyone Has Standards: They're both horrified and disgusted after catching Kevin masturbating in the store bathroom.C-Dog: He is therespanking it, man! He isindecent! Eye-Obscuring Hat: C-Dog wears one. He's never shown without it at all, meaning the viewer has no idea what his eyes looks like. Strangely, he even wears it to court, despite otherwise being dressed in a suit. Gang of Bullies: In the unproduced episodes, they're joined by an older, larger bully named "Phat Ass".

characters.name: Penis Pervert
characters.description: A minor character who for some reason walks up to random people just to repeatedly whisper the word "penis" to them. All There in the Script: The script gives his actual designation as Concerned Businessman, but most fans refer to him as the Penis Pervert due to his most notable attribute being his habit of whispering "penis" to people for no clear reason. Cloudcuckoolander: He's a total weirdo who repeatedly whispers the word "penis" to people.

characters.name: Stacy
characters.description: Jim’s receptionist at the ad agency. A sorority girl. All Guys Want Sorority Women: Averted. Andy doesn’t want her; he wants alt-rock girl Gwen. What Could Have Been: Doesn’t get to do much in her two appearances that were completed, but has a drunken one-night stand with Andy in an unfinished episode.
